name: pweb-csp-enforced
description: P4 deliverable (ADR 0044): the built bundle ships the CSP meta tag AND the browser ENFORCES it. Asserts the meta exists carrying EXACTLY the frozen ADR 0044 policy (exact string match — the per-directive substring checks alone are prefix-tolerant and would miss a loosening token appended to a directive, e.g. 'unsafe-eval' onto script-src), then PROVES enforcement by injecting an external cross-origin <script src=https://example.com/...> (blocked by script-src 'self') and listening for the securitypolicyviolation event — presence alone is not enforcement (a meta the browser ignores would pass a presence-only check); the fired violation is the real proof. The blocked script never hits the network (CSP is checked pre-fetch), so this is offline-safe. The CSP is build-only (apply:'build'), so this test is meaningful ONLY on the served-dist (preview) leg — the dev-web leg ships no meta. Runs last (its deliberate violation logs one console error).

commands: (async () => { const csp = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('meta')).find(m => (m.httpEquiv || '').toLowerCase() === 'content-security-policy'); if (!csp) throw new Error('CSP meta tag ABSENT from the served bundle — vite-plugin-csp did not inject (build-only; is this the preview leg?)'); const content = csp.getAttribute('content') || ''; const required = ["default-src 'self'", "script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'", "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'", "connect-src 'self' https:", "worker-src 'self' blob:", "object-src 'none'", "base-uri 'self'", "form-action 'self'"]; const missing = required.filter(d => content.indexOf(d) === -1); if (missing.length) throw new Error('CSP meta present but missing directives: ' + missing.join(' | ') + ' — full policy: ' + content); const EXPECT = "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; media-src 'self' blob:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https:; worker-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'"; if (content.trim() !== EXPECT) throw new Error('CSP POLICY DRIFT: the shipped policy no longer EXACTLY matches the frozen ADR 0044 policy — the per-directive checks above are prefix-tolerant (a token APPENDED to a directive, e.g. unsafe-eval onto script-src, would slip past them), so this exact match is the loosening tripwire. A deliberate policy change must re-prove on the served build and update this test + vite-plugin-csp.ts + ADR 0044 together. shipped: ' + content); let violation = null; const onV = (e) => { const d = (e.effectiveDirective || e.violatedDirective || ''); if (d.indexOf('script-src') === 0 && !violation) violation = { directive: d, blocked: e.blockedURI }; }; document.addEventListener('securitypolicyviolation', onV); try { const s = document.createElement('script'); s.src = 'https://example.com/canary-csp-probe.js'; document.head.appendChild(s); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000)); s.remove(); } finally { document.removeEventListener('securitypolicyviolation', onV); } if (!violation) throw new Error('CSP NOT ENFORCED: an external https script injection did NOT raise a script-src securitypolicyviolation — the meta is present but the browser is ignoring it'); return 'pass: CSP meta shipped + ENFORCED (script-src violation fired for ' + violation.blocked + ')'; })()
